title: Untitled
research_problem: The ever-increasing number of research articles makes manual literature review time-consuming, tedious, and error-prone. The primary objective is to develop a system that can automatically generate literature review segments from PDF files using NLP techniques and LLMs.
method_summary: Three approaches are implemented and compared: (1) frequency-based approach using spaCy for extractive summarization, (2) transformer-based Simple T5 model for abstractive summarization trained on SciTLDR, and (3) retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) using GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 with the SciTLDR dataset as knowledge base.
datasets: SciTLDR dataset (Hugging Face) - 5,400 TLDRs from over 3,200 papers containing abstracts, introductions, conclusions, and summaries

main_claims.claim_text: The LLM-based approach (GPT-3.5-turbo) achieved the highest ROUGE-1 score of 0.364, outperforming T5 (0.268) and spaCy (0.257)
All three NLP approaches (spaCy, T5, and GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125) can produce satisfactory results in automating literature review generation
The transformer-based T5 model ranks second in performance with ROUGE-1 of 0.268, while spaCy ranks last with ROUGE-1 of 0.257
Large language models are proven superior to older NLP approaches for literature review generation based on ROUGE-N scores

limitations.text: Models are not yet fully capable of automating literature reviews independently and require human reviewers
The research uses only the SciTLDR dataset, limiting generalizability to other domains
The spaCy approach has the lowest performance among the three methods with ROUGE-1 of 0.257

future_work: Add more functionality to the GUI such as model options and output size controls
Experiment with additional models including BERT, Gemini, and LLaMA to find better results
Enhance the effectiveness and applicability of the developed system tool
notes: First procedure uses spaCy library with word frequency calculation for sentence weighting; Second procedure uses Simple T5 model fine-tuned on SciTLDR dataset; Third procedure uses GPT-3.5-TURBO-0125 with RAG approach and custom prompt engineering; Final system includes a GUI where users can upload multiple PDFs and receive generated literature review; User evaluation was performed with 4 IoT/Healthcare research articles demonstrating coherent output generation
